shloka.description: Shloka 4.8: For the protection of the virtuous, the destruction of the wicked and the establishment of dharma, I appear again and again in every yuga.
shloka.name: For the protection of the virtuous, the destruction of the wicked and the establishment of dharma, I appear again and again in every yuga.
shloka.title: Shloka 4.8

post.title: Subscribe: Bhagavad Gita For All Youtube Channel
post.name: Be a part of our community on YouTube. Tap this message to Subscribe! 💫

post.title: Join our growing Gita-inspired community on Instagram 🪷
post.name: Tap this message to Follow our Channel & Start your journey!

latestQuiz.name: Dhritarashtra said - O Sanjaya, assembled in the holy land of Kurukshetra and desiring to fight, what did my sons and the Pandavas do?
latestQuiz.title: Shloka 1.1

shlokas.levelName: Mind is the best friend of one who has conquered it, but mind is the greatest enemy of one who has failed to conquer it.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.6: Mind is the best friend of one who has conquered it, but mind is the greatest enemy of one who has failed to conquer it.
shlokas.sanskritText:
bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ
anātmanas tu śhatrutve vartetātmaiva śhatru-vat
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.6
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: To practice Yoga, one should prepare a seat in a clean area, choosing a spot that is neither too high nor too low. Layer the seat with Kusa grass, then a deer skin and finally, cover it with a soft cloth. The yogi should then sit firmly on this seat.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.11 (11-12): To practice Yoga, one should prepare a seat in a clean area, choosing a spot that is neither too high nor too low. Layer the seat with Kusa grass, then a deer skin and finally, cover it with a soft cloth. The yogi should then sit firmly on this seat.
shlokas.sanskritText:
śhuchau deśhe pratiṣhṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ
nātyuchchhritam nāti-nīcham chailājina-kuśhottaram
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.11 (6.11 to 6.12)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.26: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśham nayet
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.26
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: O Krishna, indeed, the mind is restless, turbulent, strong and obstinate. I find controlling this mind to be more difficult than trying to control the wind.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.34: O Krishna, indeed, the mind is restless, turbulent, strong and obstinate. I find controlling this mind to be more difficult than trying to control the wind.
shlokas.sanskritText:
chañchalam hi manaḥ kṛiṣhṇa pramāthi balavad dṛiḍham
tasyāham nigraham manye vāyor iva su-duṣhkaram
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.34
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O mighty-armed! Undoubtedly, the mind is truly difficult to control due to its flickering nature. However, O Kaunteya, with consistent practice and detachment, it can be controlled.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.35: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O mighty-armed! Undoubtedly, the mind is truly difficult to control due to its flickering nature. However, O Kaunteya, with consistent practice and detachment, it can be controlled.
shlokas.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
asanśhayam mahā-bāho mano durnigraham chalam
abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa cha gṛihyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.35
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: Yoga is challenging for those whose mind is unrestrained. However, those who have learned to control the mind and who diligently strive through proper means can achieve perfection in yoga. This is My opinion.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.36: Yoga is challenging for those whose mind is unrestrained. However, those who have learned to control the mind and who diligently strive through proper means can achieve perfection in yoga. This is My opinion.
shlokas.sanskritText:
asaṅyatātmanā yogo duṣhprāpa iti me matiḥ
vaśhyātmanā tu yatatā śhakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.36
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - O Krishna, what is the fate of an unsuccessful yogi who started on this path with faith but who later deviated because of the restless mind, thereby failing to attain the ultimate perfection of yoga?
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.37: Arjuna said - O Krishna, what is the fate of an unsuccessful yogi who started on this path with faith but who later deviated because of the restless mind, thereby failing to attain the ultimate perfection of yoga?
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
ayatiḥ śhraddhayopeto yogāch chalita-mānasaḥ
aprāpya yoga-sansiddhim kāṅ gatim kṛiṣhṇa gachchhati
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.37 (6.37 to 6.39)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: When your intellect is no more attracted to the fruitive sections of the Vedas and when it remains steadfast in a state of samadhi, then it attains the state of Yoga.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.53: When your intellect is no more attracted to the fruitive sections of the Vedas and when it remains steadfast in a state of samadhi, then it attains the state of Yoga.
shlokas.sanskritText:
śhruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niśhchalā
samādhāv-achalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.53
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Therefore, by controlling all the senses and practicing yoga, one should fix his consciousness upon Me . For one who has mastered control over the senses, he is to be considered firmly established in perfect wisdom.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.61: Therefore, by controlling all the senses and practicing yoga, one should fix his consciousness upon Me. For one who has mastered control over the senses, he is to be considered firmly established in perfect wisdom.
shlokas.sanskritText:
tāni sarvāṇi sanyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ
vaśhe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.61
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Conversely, one who is not connected with the Supreme, cannot have stable intelligence or a fixed mind. Without them there cannot be any peace. And for one who lacks peace, there is no question of happiness too.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.66: Conversely, one who is not connected with the Supreme, cannot have stable intelligence or a fixed mind. Without them there cannot be any peace. And for one who lacks peace, there is no question of happiness too.
shlokas.sanskritText:
nāsti buddhir-ayuktasya na chāyuktasya bhāvanā
na chābhāvayataḥ śhāntir aśhāntasya kutaḥ sukham
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.66
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: O scion of Bharata! Whenever there is a decline in dharma and a predominance of adharma, at that time I manifest myself.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.7: O scion of Bharata! Whenever there is a decline in dharma and a predominance of adharma, at that time I manifest myself.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānam sṛijāmyaham
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.7
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: For the protection of the virtuous, the destruction of the wicked and the establishment of dharma, I appear again and again in every yuga.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.8: For the protection of the virtuous, the destruction of the wicked and the establishment of dharma, I appear again and again in every yuga.
shlokas.sanskritText:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnām vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitām
dharma-sansthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.8
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: The omnipresent Supreme Self neither partakes in the sins nor the merits of anyone. It is the veil of ignorance that covers the real knowledge and bewilders the embodied beings.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.15: The omnipresent Supreme Self neither partakes in the sins nor the merits of anyone. It is the veil of ignorance that covers the real knowledge and bewilders the embodied beings.
shlokas.sanskritText:
nādatte kasyachit pāpam na chaiva sukṛitam vibhuḥ
ajñānenāvṛitam jñānam tena muhyanti jantavaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.15
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: O Kaunteya! Pleasures born of contact with external sense objects are sources of suffering. Such pleasures are transient in nature, with a beginning and an end. Thus, the wise do not indulge in them.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.22: O Kaunteya! Pleasures born of contact with external sense objects are sources of suffering. Such pleasures are transient in nature, with a beginning and an end. Thus, the wise do not indulge in them.
shlokas.sanskritText:
ye hi sansparśha-jā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te
ādyantavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣhu ramate budhaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.22
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: O Lord Vishnu! Beholding Your radiant and multicolored form stretching to the sky, with Your mouth wide open and large glowing eyes, I am disturbed internally, and I am unable to keep my mind steady or calm.
shlokas.description: Shloka 11.24: O Lord Vishnu! Beholding Your radiant and multicolored form stretching to the sky, with Your mouth wide open and large glowing eyes, I am disturbed internally, and I am unable to keep my mind steady or calm.
shlokas.sanskritText:
nabhaḥ-spṛiśham dīptam aneka-varṇam
vyāttānanam dīpta-viśhāla-netram
dṛiṣhṭvā hi tvām pravyathitāntar-ātmā
dhṛitim na vindāmi śhamam cha viṣhṇo
shlokas.title: Shloka 11.24 (11.24 to 11.30)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision

shlokas.levelName: Wise men of stable intelligence, renounce attachment to the fruits of their actions and thus liberate themselves from the cycle of birth and death and attain the supreme state free of any misery.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.51: Wise men of stable intelligence, renounce attachment to the fruits of their actions and thus liberate themselves from the cycle of birth and death and attain the supreme state free of any misery.
shlokas.sanskritText:
karma-jam buddhi-yuktā hi phalam tyaktvā manīṣhiṇaḥ
janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padam gachchhanty-anāmayam
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.51
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Upon achieving such grace, all sorrows end. And in a happy state of mind, one’s intellect becomes completely fixed.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.65: Upon achieving such grace, all sorrows end. And in a happy state of mind, one’s intellect becomes completely fixed.
shlokas.sanskritText:
prasāde sarva-duḥkhānām hānir asyopajāyate
prasanna-chetaso hyāśhu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣhṭhate
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.65
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: One should elevate oneself with the help of the mind and avoid degrading oneself. The mind can either be your friend or your foe.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.5: One should elevate oneself with the help of the mind and avoid degrading oneself. The mind can either be your friend or your foe.
shlokas.sanskritText:
uddhared ātmanātmānam nātmānam avasādayet
ātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.5
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: O scion of Bharata! Whenever there is a decline in dharma and a predominance of adharma, at that time I manifest myself.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.7: O scion of Bharata! Whenever there is a decline in dharma and a predominance of adharma, at that time I manifest myself.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānam sṛijāmyaham
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.7
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: Therefore, always remember Me and perform your duty of fighting this war. With mind and intelligence fixed on Me, you will undoubtedly come to Me.
shlokas.description: Shloka 8.7: Therefore, always remember Me and perform your duty of fighting this war. With mind and intelligence fixed on Me, you will undoubtedly come to Me.
shlokas.sanskritText:
tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu mām anusmara yudhya cha
mayyarpita-mano-buddhir mām evaiṣhyasyasanśhayam
shlokas.title: Shloka 8.7
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 8: Akshara Brahma Yoga - The Yoga of the Imperishable Brahman

shlokas.levelName: A person who performs actions without attachment and offers the results of one’s work unto the Supreme, remains untouched by any sinful reaction, just like a lotus leaf remains untouched by water.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.10: A person who performs actions without attachment and offers the results of one’s work unto the Supreme, remains untouched by any sinful reaction, just like a lotus leaf remains untouched by water.
shlokas.sanskritText:
brahmaṇyādhāya karmāṇi saṅgam tyaktvā karoti yaḥ
lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.10
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: O Kaunteya! The fleeting nature of happiness and distress, which come and go like the changing winter and summer seasons, are rooted in sense perception. One must learn to tolerate them, without being disturbed, O scion of Bharata!
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.14: O Kaunteya! The fleeting nature of happiness and distress, which come and go like the changing winter and summer seasons, are rooted in sense perception. One must learn to tolerate them, without being disturbed, O scion of Bharata!
shlokas.sanskritText:
mātrā-sparśhās tu kaunteya śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ
āgamāpāyino ’nityās tāns-titikṣhasva bhārata
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.14
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: He who is unperturbed by others and does not provoke others and who remains free from joy, envy, fear and anxiety– such a person is very dear to Me.
shlokas.description: Shloka 12.15: He who is unperturbed by others and does not provoke others and who remains free from joy, envy, fear and anxiety– such a person is very dear to Me.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate cha yaḥ
harṣhāmarṣha-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa cha me priyaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 12.15
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 12: Bhakti Yoga - The Yoga of Devotion

shlokas.levelName: The soul is never born, nor does it ever die; nor does it come into being with the creation of the body and neither does it ever cease to exist. It is birthless, eternal, immortal and timeless. The soul cannot be destroyed, even if the body is slain.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.20: The soul is never born, nor does it ever die; nor does it come into being with the creation of the body and neither does it ever cease to exist. It is birthless, eternal, immortal and timeless. The soul cannot be destroyed, even if the body is slain.
shlokas.sanskritText:
na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin
nāyam bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yam purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.20
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: The Purusha situated within Prakriti, experiences the material qualities (gunas) arising from Prakriti. Attachment to these material qualities or gunas is the cause of birth in superior or inferior wombs.
shlokas.description: Shloka 13.22: The Purusha situated within Prakriti, experiences the material qualities (gunas) arising from Prakriti. Attachment to these material qualities or gunas is the cause of birth in superior or inferior wombs.
shlokas.sanskritText:
puruṣhaḥ prakṛiti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛiti-jān guṇān
kāraṇam guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu
shlokas.title: Shloka 13.22
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 13: Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field

shlokas.levelName: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.26: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśham nayet
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.26
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: Surrendering all your actions unto Me, with your mind fixed on the knowledge of the self, free from desires and possessiveness, dispel the fever of confusion and engage in battle.
shlokas.description: Shloka 3.30: Surrendering all your actions unto Me, with your mind fixed on the knowledge of the self, free from desires and possessiveness, dispel the fever of confusion and engage in battle.
shlokas.sanskritText:
mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā
nirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 3.30
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 3: Karma Yoga - The Yoga of Righteous Action

shlokas.levelName: You have the right to perform your actions, but you have no rights over the results of those actions. Do not attribute the outcome solely to yourself, nor be attached to not performing your duties.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.47: You have the right to perform your actions, but you have no rights over the results of those actions. Do not attribute the outcome solely to yourself, nor be attached to not performing your duties.
shlokas.sanskritText:
karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana
mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.47
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Equipped with deliberate intelligence, one who works without attachment to the results, can transcend the effects of both good and bad actions in this birth itself. Therefore, you should cultivate this Yoga, which is the art of skillful work.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.50: Equipped with deliberate intelligence, one who works without attachment to the results, can transcend the effects of both good and bad actions in this birth itself. Therefore, you should cultivate this Yoga, which is the art of skillful work.
shlokas.sanskritText:
buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛita-duṣhkṛite
tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśhalam
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.50
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - Having received this profoundly confidential spiritual knowledge, that You have revealed out of compassion for me, my ignorance is now dispelled.
shlokas.description: Shloka 11.1: Arjuna said - Having received this profoundly confidential spiritual knowledge, that You have revealed out of compassion for me, my ignorance is now dispelled.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
mad-anugrahāya paramam guhyam adhyātma-sanjñitam
yat tvayoktam vachas tena moho ’yam vigato mama
shlokas.title: Shloka 11.1 (11.1 to 11.4)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision

shlokas.levelName: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! Now see hundreds and thousands of variations of My manifold celestial forms, which are of various colours and shapes.
shlokas.description: Shloka 11.5: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - O Partha! Now see hundreds and thousands of variations of My manifold celestial forms, which are of various colours and shapes.
shlokas.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
paśhya me pārtha rūpāṇi śhataśho ’tha sahasraśhaḥ
nānā-vidhāni divyāni nānā-varṇākṛitīni cha
shlokas.title: Shloka 11.5 (11.5 to 11.7)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision

shlokas.levelName: But you cannot see Me with your physical eyes. Therefore, I will give you divine eyes. Now watch My divine mystic powers.
shlokas.description: Shloka 11.8: But you cannot see Me with your physical eyes. Therefore, I will give you divine eyes. Now watch My divine mystic powers.
shlokas.sanskritText:
na tu mām śhakyase draṣhṭum anenaiva sva-chakṣhuṣhā
divyam dadāmi te chakṣhuḥ paśhya me yogam aiśhwaram
shlokas.title: Shloka 11.8
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision

shlokas.levelName: O Arjuna! Whoever truly understands the divine nature of My birth and activities does not take birth again in this material world after leaving the body, but instead attains Me.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.9: O Arjuna! Whoever truly understands the divine nature of My birth and activities does not take birth again in this material world after leaving the body, but instead attains Me.
shlokas.sanskritText:
janma karma cha me divyam evam yo vetti tattvataḥ
tyaktvā deham punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.9
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: Fools are unable to recognise Me when I appear in the human form. They are unable to perceive the transcendental, divine nature of My personality as the Supreme Lord of all beings.
shlokas.description: Shloka 9.11: Fools are unable to recognise Me when I appear in the human form. They are unable to perceive the transcendental, divine nature of My personality as the Supreme Lord of all beings.
shlokas.sanskritText:
avajānanti mām mūḍhā mānuṣhīm tanum āśhritam
param bhāvam ajānanto mama bhūta-maheśhvaram
shlokas.title: Shloka 9.11
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 9: Raja Vidya Rajaguhya Yoga - The Yoga of Supreme Knowledge and Mystery

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode and the Supremely Pure. You are the eternal, divine, original, unborn and the greatest person.
shlokas.description: Shloka 10.12: Arjuna said - You are the Supreme Brahman, the Supreme Abode and the Supremely Pure. You are the eternal, divine, original, unborn and the greatest person.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
param brahma param dhāma pavitram paramam bhavān
puruṣham śhāśhvatam divyam ādi-devam ajam vibhum
shlokas.title: Shloka 10.12 (10.12 to 10.13)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 10: Vibhuti Yoga - The Yoga of Divine Glories

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - O Lord! I see all the devatas and various other living beings gathered within Your divine form. I see Brahma seated on the lotus, Shiva, the great sages and the divine serpents.
shlokas.description: Shloka 11.15: Arjuna said - O Lord! I see all the devatas and various other living beings gathered within Your divine form. I see Brahma seated on the lotus, Shiva, the great sages and the divine serpents.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
paśhyāmi devāns tava deva dehe
sarvāns tathā bhūta-viśheṣha-saṅghān
brahmāṇam īśham kamalāsana-stham
ṛiṣhīnśh cha sarvān uragānśh cha divyān
shlokas.title: Shloka 11.15 (11.15 to 11.18)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision

shlokas.levelName: A humble person, fully equipped with divine knowledge, views all beings with an equal vision, whether they be a learned Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, or an outcaste.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.18: A humble person, fully equipped with divine knowledge, views all beings with an equal vision, whether they be a learned Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, or an outcaste.
shlokas.sanskritText:
vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini
śhuni chaiva śhva-pāke cha paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśhinaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.18
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: Unintelligent men think that I was formless before and have now taken this form. They do not know My highest nature which is changeless and is the best.
shlokas.description: Shloka 7.24: Unintelligent men think that I was formless before and have now taken this form. They do not know My highest nature which is changeless and is the best.
shlokas.sanskritText:
avyaktam vyaktim āpannam manyante mām abuddhayaḥ
param bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam
shlokas.title: Shloka 7.24
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 7: Gyana Vigyana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom

shlokas.levelName: One who is united with the Supreme through yoga, perceives with an equal vision, the Supreme in all living beings and sees all living beings in the Supreme.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.29: One who is united with the Supreme through yoga, perceives with an equal vision, the Supreme in all living beings and sees all living beings in the Supreme.
shlokas.sanskritText:
sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānam sarva-bhūtāni chātmani
īkṣhate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśhanaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.29
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - O Krishna! You have praised the path of sannyasa, renunciation of action and also the path of Karma Yoga the path of action without attachment to the results. But between these two, which one is superior? Please tell me definitively.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.1: Arjuna said - O Krishna! You have praised the path of sannyasa, renunciation of action and also the path of Karma Yoga the path of action without attachment to the results. But between these two, which one is superior? Please tell me definitively.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
sannyāsam karmaṇām kṛiṣhṇa punar yogam cha śhansasi
yach chhreya etayor ekam tan me brūhi su-niśhchitam
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.1
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Sannyasa, renunciation of action and Karma Yoga, action without attachment to results, both lead to the path of liberation. However, between the two, the path of Karma Yoga is better than Sannyasa.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - Sannyasa, renunciation of action and Karma Yoga, action without attachment to results, both lead to the path of liberation. However, between the two, the path of Karma Yoga is better than Sannyasa.
shlokas.sanskritText:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
sannyāsaḥ karma-yogaśh cha niḥśhreyasa-karāvubhau
tayos tu karma-sannyāsāt karma-yogo viśhiṣhyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.2
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - According to Me, the devotees who always worship Me with divine faith and fix their minds on Me, are the highest and the most esteemed.
shlokas.description: Shloka 12.2: Bhagawan Sri Krishna said - According to Me, the devotees who always worship Me with divine faith and fix their minds on Me, are the highest and the most esteemed.
shlokas.sanskritText:
śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
mayy āveśhya mano ye mām nitya-yuktā upāsate
śhraddhayā parayopetās te me yuktatamā matāḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 12.2
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 12: Bhakti Yoga - The Yoga of Devotion

shlokas.levelName: Nor do I know whether it would be better if we defeat them (the Kauravas) or if they defeat us. I have no desire to live after slaying them. Yet, here stand the sons of Dhritarashtra, prepared to fight against us on this battlefield.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.6: Nor do I know whether it would be better if we defeat them (the Kauravas) or if they defeat us. I have no desire to live after slaying them. Yet, here stand the sons of Dhritarashtra, prepared to fight against us on this battlefield.
shlokas.sanskritText:
na chaitadvidmaḥ kataranno garīyo
yadvā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥ
yāneva hatvā na jijīviṣhāmas
te ’vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣhṭrāḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.6
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: To those who are always devoted to Me and worship Me with love, I give them the knowledge by which they can come to Me.
shlokas.description: Shloka 10.10: To those who are always devoted to Me and worship Me with love, I give them the knowledge by which they can come to Me.
shlokas.sanskritText:
teṣhām satata-yuktānām bhajatām prīti-pūrvakam
dadāmi buddhi-yogam tam yena mām upayānti te
shlokas.title: Shloka 10.10
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 10: Vibhuti Yoga - The Yoga of Divine Glories

shlokas.levelName: Intellect and determination are also categorised into three types based on the gunas. O Dhananjaya, listen to them carefully as I describe them distinctly and in detail.
shlokas.description: Shloka 18.29: Intellect and determination are also categorised into three types based on the gunas. O Dhananjaya, listen to them carefully as I describe them distinctly and in detail.
shlokas.sanskritText:
buddher bhedam dhṛiteśh chaiva guṇatas tri-vidham śhṛiṇu
prochyamānam aśheṣheṇa pṛithaktvena dhanañjaya
shlokas.title: Shloka 18.29 (29 to 32)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 18: Moksha Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Liberation and Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: Only sin will befall upon us if we slay these warmongers. O Madhava! Therefore it would not be righteous for us to kill the sons of Dhritarashtra and our other kinsmen. How could we find happiness by murdering our own relatives?
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.36: Only sin will befall upon us if we slay these warmongers. O Madhava! Therefore it would not be righteous for us to kill the sons of Dhritarashtra and our other kinsmen. How could we find happiness by murdering our own relatives?
shlokas.sanskritText:
pāpam evāśhrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥ
tasmān nārhā vayaṁ hantuṁ dhārtarāṣhṭrān sa-bāndhavān
sva-janaṁ hi kathaṁ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.36
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: If you die in this righteous war, you will attain heaven. If you emerge victorious, you will rule over the world. Therefore, arise, Arjuna, be determined and prepare yourself for battle.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.37: : If you die in this righteous war, you will attain heaven. If you emerge victorious, you will rule over the world. Therefore, arise, Arjuna, be determined and prepare yourself for battle.
shlokas.sanskritText:
hato vā prāpsyasi swargam jitvā vā bhokṣhyase mahīm
tasmād uttiṣhṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛita-niśhchayaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.37
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who neither hates nor desires the results of actions should be considered as always renounced. He is unaffected by all dualities, and easily achieves liberation from bondage.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.3: O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who neither hates nor desires the results of actions should be considered as always renounced. He is unaffected by all dualities, and easily achieves liberation from bondage.
shlokas.sanskritText:
jñeyaḥ sa nitya-sannyāsī yo na dveṣhṭi na kāṅkṣhati
nirdvandvo hi mahā-bāho sukham bandhāt pramuchyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.3
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: Know that all living beings come from these two natures. And I am both the origin and end of the entire universe.
shlokas.description: Shloka 7.6: Know that all living beings come from these two natures. And I am both the origin and end of the entire universe.
shlokas.sanskritText:
etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇītyupadhāraya
aham kṛitsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā
shlokas.title: Shloka 7.6
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 7: Gyana Vigyana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom

shlokas.levelName: Freed from attachment, fear and anger, being fully absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many, many people in the past have attained My supreme nature after being purified by this austerity of knowledge.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.10: Freed from attachment, fear and anger, being fully absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many, many people in the past have attained My supreme nature after being purified by this austerity of knowledge.
shlokas.sanskritText:
vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhā man-mayā mām upāśhritāḥ
bahavo jñāna-tapasā pūtā mad-bhāvam āgatāḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.10
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: O Kaunteya! The fleeting nature of happiness and distress, which come and go like the changing winter and summer seasons, are rooted in sense perception. One must learn to tolerate them, without being disturbed, O scion of Bharata!
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.14: O Kaunteya! The fleeting nature of happiness and distress, which come and go like the changing winter and summer seasons, are rooted in sense perception. One must learn to tolerate them, without being disturbed, O scion of Bharata!
shlokas.sanskritText:
mātrā-sparśhās tu kaunteya śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ
āgamāpāyino ’nityās tāns-titikṣhasva bhārata
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.14
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: O best among humans! One who remains unaffected by these fluctuations, maintaining equanimity in sorrow and happiness, becomes qualified for immortality.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.15: O best among humans! One who remains unaffected by these fluctuations, maintaining equanimity in sorrow and happiness, becomes qualified for immortality.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yam hi na vyathayantyete puruṣham puruṣharṣhabha
sama-duḥkha-sukham dhīram so ’mṛitatvāya kalpate
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.15
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: The unreal has no true existence and the real is eternal and cannot be non-existent. This understanding of the nature of these two has been realized by the wise seers of truth.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.16: The unreal has no true existence and the real is eternal and cannot be non-existent. This understanding of the nature of these two has been realized by the wise seers of truth.
shlokas.sanskritText:
nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ
ubhayorapi dṛiṣhṭo ’nta stvanayos tattva-darśhibhiḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.16
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: O scion of Bharata! O Parantapa! All the living beings are born deluded, bewildered by dualities arising from desire and hatred.
shlokas.description: Shloka 7.27: O scion of Bharata! O Parantapa! All the living beings are born deluded, bewildered by dualities arising from desire and hatred.
shlokas.sanskritText:
ichchhā-dveṣha-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata
sarva-bhūtāni sammoham sarge yānti parantapa
shlokas.title: Shloka 7.27
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 7: Gyana Vigyana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - Looking at these people who are my kin gathered here poised to fight against each other in the battle, O Krishna, my limbs have started to shake and my mouth is getting parched.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.28 (28-29): Arjuna said - Looking at these people who are my kin gathered here poised to fight against each other in the battle, O Krishna, my limbs have started to shake and my mouth is getting parched.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
dṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitam
sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ cha pariśhuṣhyati
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.28 (1.28 to 1.29)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: But an enlightened person, O mighty-armed one, understands that it’s only the senses which are engaged with the sense objects, and both are products of the gunas. Thus, understanding that it’s only the gunas which are active, they do not become attached.
shlokas.description: Shloka 3.28: But an enlightened person, O mighty-armed one, understands that it’s only the senses which are engaged with the sense objects, and both are products of the gunas. Thus, understanding that it’s only the gunas which are active, they do not become attached.
shlokas.sanskritText:
tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ
guṇā guṇeṣhu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate
shlokas.title: Shloka 3.28
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 3: Karma Yoga - The Yoga of Righteous Action

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna having said this collapsed onto the seat of his chariot setting aside his bow and arrows, his mind overwhelmed with grief.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.46: Arjuna having said this collapsed onto the seat of his chariot setting aside his bow and arrows, his mind overwhelmed with grief.
shlokas.sanskritText:
sañjaya uvāca 
evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśat 
visṛjya sa-śaraṁ cāpaṁ śoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.46
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: Although I am birthless and have an imperishable body and I am also the Lord of all living beings, yet I manifest Myself through My divine potency.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.6: Although I am birthless and have an imperishable body and I am also the Lord of all living beings, yet I manifest Myself through My divine potency.
shlokas.sanskritText:
ajo ’pi sannavyayātmā bhūtānām īśhvaro ’pi san
prakṛitim svām adhiṣhṭhāya sambhavāmyātma-māyayā
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.6
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: There was never a time when I, you and all these kings did not exist in the past. Similarly, there will never be a time in the future when we will cease to exist.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.12: There was never a time when I, you and all these kings did not exist in the past. Similarly, there will never be a time in the future when we will cease to exist.
shlokas.sanskritText:
na tvevāham jātu nāsam na tvam neme janādhipāḥ
na chaiva na bhaviṣhyāmaḥ sarve vayamataḥ param
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.12
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Just as the embodied soul passes through various stages of life within the body—childhood, youth and old age—similarly, the soul transmigrates to another body after death. Understanding this truth, the self-realised soul does not become bewildered by such a change.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.13: Just as the embodied soul passes through various stages of life within the body—childhood, youth and old age—similarly, the soul transmigrates to another body after death. Understanding this truth, the self-realised soul does not become bewildered by such a change.
shlokas.sanskritText:
dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāram yauvanam jarā
tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.13
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: I shall reveal to you what should be known, by knowing which one attains immortality.It is the beginningless Brahman, which is subordinate to Me and which transcends both existence and non-existence.
shlokas.description: Shloka 13.13: I shall reveal to you what should be known, by knowing which one attains immortality.It is the beginningless Brahman, which is subordinate to Me and which transcends both existence and non-existence.
shlokas.sanskritText:
jñeyam yat tat pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛitam aśhnute
anādi mat-param brahma na sat tan nāsad uchyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 13.13
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 13: Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field

shlokas.levelName: You should know that which pervades the entire body is imperishable. Nothing can ever cause the destruction of this eternal being.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.17 (17-18): You should know that which pervades the entire body is imperishable. Nothing can ever cause the destruction of this eternal being.
shlokas.sanskritText:
avināśhi tu tadviddhi yena sarvam idam tatam
vināśham avyayasyāsya na kaśhchit kartum arhati
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.17 (2.17 to 2.18)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: Those who believe the soul can kill or be killed are equally unaware of the truth, because the soul neither kills nor is killed.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.19: Those who believe the soul can kill or be killed are equally unaware of the truth, because the soul neither kills nor is killed.
shlokas.sanskritText:
ya enam vetti hantāram yaśh chainam manyate hatam
ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyam hanti na hanyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.19
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: The soul is never born, nor does it ever die; nor does it come into being with the creation of the body and neither does it ever cease to exist. It is birthless, eternal, immortal and timeless. The soul cannot be destroyed, even if the body is slain.
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.20: The soul is never born, nor does it ever die; nor does it come into being with the creation of the body and neither does it ever cease to exist. It is birthless, eternal, immortal and timeless. The soul cannot be destroyed, even if the body is slain.
shlokas.sanskritText:
na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin
nāyam bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yam purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.20
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: O Partha! One who understands the soul as eternal, unborn, undecaying and indestructible—how can such a person cause death to anyone or instigate anyone else to kill?
shlokas.description: Shloka 2.21: O Partha! One who understands the soul as eternal, unborn, undecaying and indestructible—how can such a person cause death to anyone or instigate anyone else to kill?
shlokas.sanskritText:
vedāvināśhinam nityam ya enam ajam avyayam
katham sa puruṣhaḥ pārtha kam ghātayati hanti kam
shlokas.title: Shloka 2.21
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 2: Sankhya Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and  Discernment

shlokas.levelName: I shall now explain to you, O best of the Bharatas, the different times at which a yogi departs from this world – one leading to liberation and the other leading to rebirth.
shlokas.description: Shloka 8.23: I shall now explain to you, O best of the Bharatas, the different times at which a yogi departs from this world – one leading to liberation and the other leading to rebirth.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yatra kāle tvanāvṛittim āvṛittim chaiva yoginaḥ
prayātā yānti tam kālam vakṣhyāmi bharatarṣhabha
shlokas.title: Shloka 8.23 (8.23 to 8.26)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 8: Akshara Brahma Yoga - The Yoga of the Imperishable Brahman

shlokas.levelName: O best of the Bharatas, know that whatever being is born, whether moving or non-moving, arises from the union of the field (kshetra) and the knower of the field (kshetrajna).
shlokas.description: Shloka 13.27: O best of the Bharatas, know that whatever being is born, whether moving or non-moving, arises from the union of the field (kshetra) and the knower of the field (kshetrajna).
shlokas.sanskritText: yāvat sañjāyate kiñchit sattvam sthāvara-jaṅgamam
shlokas.title: Shloka 13.27 (13.27 to 13.29)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 13: Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga - The Yoga of the Field and the Knower of the Field

shlokas.levelName: Here in this army are many heroes, who are mighty archers and are equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna and great chariot warriors like Yuyudhana, Virata and Drupada.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.4 (4-6): Here in this army are many heroes, who are mighty archers and are equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna and great chariot warriors like Yuyudhana, Virata and Drupada.
shlokas.sanskritText:
atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhi
yuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.4 (1.4 to 1.6)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: Our army, completely protected by the great warrior Bhishma is unlimited in strength, while their army carefully secured by Bhima is limited in strength.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.10: Our army, completely protected by the great warrior Bhishma is unlimited in strength, while their army carefully secured by Bhima is limited in strength.
shlokas.sanskritText:
aparyāptaṁ tadasmākaṁ balaṁ bhīṣhmābhirakṣhitam
paryāptaṁ tvidameteṣhāṁ balaṁ bhīmābhirakṣhitam
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.10
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: At the time of death, one who fixes the life air in between the eyebrows and by the power of yoga with a focused mind remembers the Supreme Divine Being with devotion certainly attains Him.
shlokas.description: Shloka 8.10: At the time of death, one who fixes the life air in between the eyebrows and by the power of yoga with a focused mind remembers the Supreme Divine Being with devotion certainly attains Him.
shlokas.sanskritText:
prayāṇa-kāle manasāchalena
bhaktyā yukto yoga-balena chaiva
bhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśhya samyak
sa tam param puruṣham upaiti divyam
shlokas.title: Shloka 8.10
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 8: Akshara Brahma Yoga - The Yoga of the Imperishable Brahman

shlokas.levelName: All of them are certainly noble, but in My opinion, I consider the enlightened ones to be like My own self. Being connected to Me, they will certainly attain Me, the highest destination.
shlokas.description: Shloka 7.18: All of them are certainly noble, but in My opinion, I consider the enlightened ones to be like My own self. Being connected to Me, they will certainly attain Me, the highest destination.
shlokas.sanskritText:
udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tvātmaiva me matam
āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamām gatim
shlokas.title: Shloka 7.18
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 7: Gyana Vigyana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom

shlokas.levelName: The collective sound was tumultuous, reverberating through the sky and the universe, it pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhritarashtra.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.19: The collective sound was tumultuous, reverberating through the sky and the universe, it pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhritarashtra.
shlokas.sanskritText:
sa ghoṣho dhārtarāṣhṭrāṇāṁ hṛidayāni vyadārayat
nabhaśhcha pṛithivīṁ chaiva tumulo abhyanunādayan
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.19
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: Sanjaya said - O scion of Bharata! As requested by Arjuna, Lord Hrishikesh steered the fine chariot between the two armies.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.24 (24-25): Sanjaya said - O scion of Bharata! As requested by Arjuna, Lord Hrishikesh steered the fine chariot between the two armies.
shlokas.sanskritText:
sañjaya uvācha
evam ukto hṛiṣhīkeśho guḍākeśhena bhārata
senayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.24 (1.24 to 1.25)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokas.description: Shloka 6.26: The mind may wander repeatedly here and there due to its restless, unsteady nature. But a yogi must regulate it and bring it back under the control of the self.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśham nayet
shlokas.title: Shloka 6.26
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 6: Dhyana Yoga - The Yoga of Self-Discipline

shlokas.levelName: O Dhananjaya! One who has renounced the fruits of one’s work, whose doubts are dispelled by divine knowledge, and who is situated in the knowledge of the self, is not bound by the results of one’s actions.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.41 (41-42): O Dhananjaya! One who has renounced the fruits of one’s work, whose doubts are dispelled by divine knowledge, and who is situated in the knowledge of the self, is not bound by the results of one’s actions.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yoga-sannyasta-karmāṇam jñāna-sañchhinna-sanśhayam
ātmavantam na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.41 (4.41 to 4.42)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: Thus, understanding the self to be superior to intellect and controlling the mind by discerning intelligence, O mighty-armed one, conquer this formidable enemy in the form of lust.
shlokas.description: Shloka 3.43: Thus, understanding the self to be superior to intellect and controlling the mind by discerning intelligence, O mighty-armed one, conquer this formidable enemy in the form of lust.
shlokas.sanskritText:
evam buddheḥ param buddhvā sanstabhyātmānam ātmanā
jahi śhatrum mahā-bāho kāma-rūpam durāsadam
shlokas.title: Shloka 3.43
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 3: Karma Yoga - The Yoga of Righteous Action

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - O Krishna! You have praised the path of sannyasa, renunciation of action and also the path of Karma Yoga the path of action without attachment to the results. But between these two, which one is superior? Please tell me definitively.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.1: Arjuna said - O Krishna! You have praised the path of sannyasa, renunciation of action and also the path of Karma Yoga the path of action without attachment to the results. But between these two, which one is superior? Please tell me definitively.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
sannyāsam karmaṇām kṛiṣhṇa punar yogam cha śhansasi
yach chhreya etayor ekam tan me brūhi su-niśhchitam
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.1
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who neither hates nor desires the results of actions should be considered as always renounced. He is unaffected by all dualities, and easily achieves liberation from bondage.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.3: O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who neither hates nor desires the results of actions should be considered as always renounced. He is unaffected by all dualities, and easily achieves liberation from bondage.
shlokas.sanskritText:
jñeyaḥ sa nitya-sannyāsī yo na dveṣhṭi na kāṅkṣhati
nirdvandvo hi mahā-bāho sukham bandhāt pramuchyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.3
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: O Kaunteya! Work binds one in this world except for the actions performed as sacrifice for the Supreme Being. Therefore without attachment to the results, you should carry out your duties as a form of sacrifice for His satisfaction.
shlokas.description: Shloka 3.9: O Kaunteya! Work binds one in this world except for the actions performed as sacrifice for the Supreme Being. Therefore without attachment to the results, you should carry out your duties as a form of sacrifice for His satisfaction.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yam karma-bandhanaḥ
tad-artham karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samāchara
shlokas.title: Shloka 3.9
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 3: Karma Yoga - The Yoga of Righteous Action

shlokas.levelName: By letting go of attachment to the fruits of actions, a yogi attains uninterrupted peace. Conversely, one who is not a yogi, who is driven by desires and is attached to the fruits of actions, he becomes bound.
shlokas.description: Shloka 5.12: By letting go of attachment to the fruits of actions, a yogi attains uninterrupted peace. Conversely, one who is not a yogi, who is driven by desires and is attached to the fruits of actions, he becomes bound.
shlokas.sanskritText:
yuktaḥ karma-phalam tyaktvā śhāntim āpnoti naiṣhṭhikīm
ayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate
shlokas.title: Shloka 5.12
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 5: Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Selfless Renunciation

shlokas.levelName: In the past people seeking liberation performed their actions with this understanding. Therefore you should also perform your duties following their example.
shlokas.description: Shloka 4.15: In the past people seeking liberation performed their actions with this understanding. Therefore you should also perform your duties following their example.
shlokas.sanskritText:
evam jñātvā kṛitam karma pūrvair api mumukṣhubhiḥ
kuru karmaiva tasmāttvam pūrvaiḥ pūrvataram kṛitam
shlokas.title: Shloka 4.15
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 4: Gyana Karma Sannyasa Yoga - The Yoga of Renunciation Through Knowledge

shlokas.levelName: There Arjuna saw his uncles, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons and grandsons as well as his mates standing in the ranks. He also saw fathers-in-law and his friends standing in both the opposing armies.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.26 (26-27): There Arjuna saw his uncles, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons and grandsons as well as his mates standing in the ranks. He also saw fathers-in-law and his friends standing in both the opposing armies.
shlokas.sanskritText:
tatrāpaśhyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝīn atha pitāmahān
āchāryān mātulān bhrātṝīn putrān pautrān sakhīṁs tathā
śhvaśhurān suhṛidaśh chaiva senayor ubhayor api
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.26 (1.26 to 1.27)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: Arjuna said - Looking at these people who are my kin gathered here poised to fight against each other in the battle, O Krishna, my limbs have started to shake and my mouth is getting parched.
shlokas.description: Shloka 1.28 (28-29): Arjuna said - Looking at these people who are my kin gathered here poised to fight against each other in the battle, O Krishna, my limbs have started to shake and my mouth is getting parched.
shlokas.sanskritText:
arjuna uvācha
dṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitam
sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ cha pariśhuṣhyati
shlokas.title: Shloka 1.28 (1.28 to 1.29)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 1: Arjuna Vishada Yoga - The Yoga of Arjuna`s Dejection

shlokas.levelName: However, those who out of envy disregard My teachings and do not follow them, know that they are devoid of all true knowledge and they are ruined.
shlokas.description: Shloka 3.32: However, those who out of envy disregard My teachings and do not follow them, know that they are devoid of all true knowledge and they are ruined.
shlokas.sanskritText:
ye tvetad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣhṭhanti me matam
sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāns tān viddhi naṣhṭān achetasaḥ
shlokas.title: Shloka 3.32
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 3: Karma Yoga - The Yoga of Righteous Action

shlokas.levelName: You are the father of all the worlds, of both moving and non moving beings. You are worthy of worship and the supreme teacher. O Lord, with unimaginable power! How can anyone in the three worlds be greater than You, when no one even matches Your might?
shlokas.description: Shloka 11.43: You are the father of all the worlds, of both moving and non moving beings. You are worthy of worship and the supreme teacher. O Lord, with unimaginable power! How can anyone in the three worlds be greater than You, when no one even matches Your might?
shlokas.sanskritText:
pitāsi lokasya charācharasya
tvam asya pūjyaśh cha gurur garīyān
na tvat-samo ’sty abhyadhikaḥ kuto ’nyo
loka-traye ’py apratima-prabhāva
shlokas.title: Shloka 11.43 (11.43 to 11.44)
shlokas.chapterName: Chapter 11: Vishwaroopa Darshana Yoga - The Yoga of the Cosmic Vision
